source: Official GGG patch notes and GGG development forum posts

Definition.text: Leech creates recovery instances from qualifying damage events and recovers Life, Mana, or Energy Shield over time.

Purpose.text: Convert a share of recently dealt damage into timed recovery and cap recovery rate to avoid unlimited sustain.

Core Rules.text: Leech effects now stack: each hit against multiple targets can grant multiple leech effects, and multiple hits against one target can create multiple instances.

Internal Rules.text: Leech follows per-instance limits plus a global pool limit: a single instance is bounded by its own maximum recovery and recover rate; the character has a maximum total leech recovery rate cap.

Calculation Order.text: 1) Hit or other qualifying event creates leech effect instance, 2) apply "Leech as" percentage to deal-to-leech amount, 3) apply per-instance cap and per-second rate, 4) apply total leech cap across active instances.

Priority Rules.text: Higher-priority per-mechanic caps (instance and total rate caps) apply before relative modifiers that scale leech gain or leech rate.

Preconditions.text: Leech source and leech pool (Life/Mana/ES) must be available to the damaging effect and be allowed by passives, gem/gear text, and modifiers.

Postconditions.text: Recovery is scheduled as timed recovery effects and can continue across multiple instances until per-instance amount or total duration/cap is consumed.

Limitations.text: Base leech values were reduced in the cited patch era and were explicitly rebalanced so that life/mana systems do not inherit legacy pre-change rates.

Caps.text: Base Maximum Life/Mana Leech Rate is 20% of pool per second; base Life/Mana Leech Rate is 2% of pool per second. Existing values were scaled down and modifiers to speed are controlled via changed leech-per-second wording.

Formula.text: leech_rate_per_instance = min(leeched_amount * duration_modifier, base_or_modified_leeched_per_second * instance_duration), capped by total_leech_rate_limit across active instances.

text: Stacking expectation changed at 2.0-era redesign; old non-stacking behavior is superseded by multiple-effect stacking implementation.

Exceptions.text: No generic exception text in current formal source set for base leech; special keystone handling (including Vaal Pact) is documented separately as part of implementation details.

Hidden Rules.text: Modifiers previously named "Leech rate" transitioned to "Life/Mana Leeched per second", preserving recovery-per-second gain while decoupling instance duration scaling.

Developer Notes.text: Stacking, base-rate balancing, and changed naming were explicitly introduced to reduce single-target exploitation while preserving multi-target efficiency.

Historical Changes.text: Life/Mana Leech was redesigned before 3.0 with stacking leech effects and explicit base leech-rate cap/rate values; later language changes shifted scaling modifiers from duration-altering to duration-preserving recovery-per-second modifiers.

text.note: Leech overhaul introduced stacking effects, rate cap, base rate values, and leech modifier relabeling behavior.
Leech remains represented in the same implementation framework in available official GGG references for this knowledge pass.

text: A player with zero recovery investment gets smaller per-instance output and low total throughput until enough leech percent and rates are added.
Multiple rapid hits create multiple independent recovery instances with global cap handling.
Leech rate modifiers only increase amount and not duration
